response: To make basic pizza dough, combine 3½ cups (450g) all-purpose flour, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp sugar, and 2¼ tsp (1 packet) active dry yeast in a bowl. Gradually mix in 1 cup (240ml) warm water (about 110°F/43°C) and 2 tbsp olive oil until a shaggy dough forms, then knead on a floured surface for 5–7 minutes until smooth and elastic. Place in an oiled bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm spot for 1–1½ hours until doubled. Punch down, divide into 2 balls, and let rest 10–15 minutes before rolling out.
